ale: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Aleut.
all: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Allar.
alley: noun. A narrow street or passageway, especially one through the middle of a block giving access to the rear of lots of buildings.
ally: verb. To unite or form a connection between (people or things), as between families by marriage, or between states by confederacy, league,
als: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tosk Albanian.
axe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ayerrerenge.
axel: noun. A jump that includes one (or more than one) complete turn and a half turn while in the air.
aye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ayere.
ays: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sorsogon Ayta.
ell: symbol. ISO 639-2 /T & ISO 639-3 language code for Greek.
eyas: noun. A young hawk or falcon in the nest, or that has not yet fledged, especially one that will be trained for falconry.
las: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gur Lama.
lase: verb. To use a laser beam on, as for cutting.
lax: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tiwa.
laxly: adverb. In a lax manner; without rigor or strictness.
lay: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lama Bai.
lea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lega-Shabunda.
leu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (New Guinea).
lex: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Luang.
ley: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lemolang.
lux: noun. In the International System of Units, the derived unit of illuminance or illumination; one lumen per square metre.
lyase: noun. Any of many classes of enzyme that catalyze the breaking of a specific form of bond
lye: noun. An alkaline liquid made by leaching ashes (usually wood ashes).
lyse: verb. To burst or cut a cell or cell structure; to induce lysis.
sae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sabanê.
sal: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Salishan languages.
sale: noun. An exchange of goods or services for currency or credit.
sau: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Saleman.
saul: noun. Archaic form of sal (“the tree, Shorea Robusta”).
sax: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sa.
say: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Saya.
sea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Semai.
seal: noun. A pinniped (Pinnipedia), particularly an earless seal (true seal) or eared seal.
sel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Selkup.
sell: verb. To transfer goods or provide services in exchange for money.
sex: noun. A category into which sexually - reproducing organisms are divided on the basis of their reproductive roles in their species; the
sexual: adjective. Arising from the fact of being male or female; pertaining to sex or gender, or to the social relations between the sexes.
sexually: adverb. In a sexual manner; erotically.
slue: verb. To rotate something on an axis.
sly: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Selayar.
sue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Suena.
sully: verb. To soil or stain; to dirty.
yea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ravula.
yell: verb. To shout; holler; make a loud sound with the voice, especially to express intense emotions such as anger or excitement.
yes: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yeskwa.
yule: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Yule.
